description: Transactional Email via Resend with feature flag, email templates, and integration into order lifecycle
steps: Add email_enabled feature flag to prisma/feature-flags.ts with config: { provider: 'resend', fromEmail: 'orders@store.com', fromName: 'Revathy Supermarket' }
Create lib/email/resend.ts - Resend API client that sends emails via fetch to https://api.resend.com/emails using RESEND_API_KEY env var. No npm package.

Create lib/email/templates.ts - Inline HTML email templates: orderConfirmation(order), orderDispatched(order, riderName), orderDelivered(order), passwordReset(otp/link). Each returns { subject, html } object.

Create lib/email/index.ts - Email service abstraction that checks email_enabled feature flag, gets config (fromEmail, fromName), and delegates to resend.ts. Exports sendOrderConfirmationEmail, sendOrderDispatchedEmail, sendOrderDeliveredEmail, sendPasswordResetEmail. All are async fire-and-forget (catch errors, log, never throw).
Integrate into app/api/orders/route.ts POST handler - After order creation in the Promise.allSettled block, add sendOrderConfirmationEmail call (needs user email from session or order data)
Add test in tests/email-templates.test.ts verifying template functions produce valid HTML with expected content
acceptance_criteria: Feature flag email_enabled exists in prisma/feature-flags.ts with enabled: false
lib/email/index.ts exports fire-and-forget email functions that check the flag
lib/email/templates.ts returns { subject, html } for all 4 template types
lib/email/resend.ts sends via fetch to Resend API, no npm dependency
Order confirmation email is triggered in POST /api/orders (fire-and-forget)
All email functions gracefully return without error when flag is disabled
verification: Run npx eslint . from apps/web/ and confirm no errors in new email files
Run npm run test from apps/web/ and confirm email template tests pass
findings: All 9 email template tests pass. Lint is clean (0 errors). Pre-existing test failure in tests/security.test.ts (production env verification - unrelated, same as FEAT-001). The order items in Prisma schema use 'name' field (not 'productName'). Email integration uses session.user.email which may be empty string if user has no email - the email service handles this gracefully by checking flag first.
